_readme: Edit this file with your real company directory. Nothing here leaves your machine -- it's read locally by router_engine.py. 'keywords' should describe the kinds of issues this person/department handles, in plain language, so the AI can match against them.

contacts.name: Aisha Mohammed
contacts.keywords: computer problems, laptop issues, monitor/dock/cable requests, password resets, software installs, VPN, wifi, printer issues, new hire equipment

contacts.name: Avan Brooks
contacts.keywords: server issues, network outages, security incidents, account access, asset inventory questions

contacts.name: Ravi Shankar
contacts.keywords: benefits, payroll questions, PTO/time off, onboarding, offboarding, workplace conflicts, policy questions, leave of absence

contacts.name: Elena Kowalski
contacts.keywords: hiring, interview scheduling, job postings, candidate questions

contacts.name: Tyler Osei
contacts.keywords: expense reports, reimbursements, invoices, budget questions, purchase orders, vendor payments

contacts.name: Sam Fitzgerald
contacts.keywords: facilities, building access/badges, desk moves, office supplies, conference room issues, deliveries, parking, cleanliness/maintenance

contacts.name: Grace Nolan
contacts.keywords: brand/logo requests, website content, social media, event swag, external communications

contacts.name: Derek Kim
contacts.keywords: CRM issues, sales tools, customer contract questions, quote requests
